# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Importing necessary libraries:
import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server/headers';  // Required if using serverless functions in Vercel ecosystem (NextJS + NXT) or any Node.js app without Serverless Functions framework and "vercel" package installed as devDependency; replace this with your actual library's name
import { PassThrough } from 'stream';  

export async function middleware(req: NextRequest): Promise<Response> {  // Assuming you are handling requests in a Node.js serverless application using Express or similar framework (replace accordingly); modify to fit the needs of your particular project setup; remove if not needed with this specific code base structure as per requirement
   try{    
      const arrayStrings: string[] = req.body();  // Assuming you are sending JSON payload in body, replace "req.body" and ["field"] for actual field names from POST data (replace accordingly); make sure to handle errors if required; this implementation assumes a successful request with no error at the edge level
      const headers: string[] = req.headers(); // Assuming you are sending JSON payload in body, replace "req.body" and ["field"] for actual field names from POST data (replace accordingly); make sure to handle errors if required; this implementation assumes a successful request with no error at the edge level
      
      const readableStream = new PassThrough();   // Create Readable Stream – We're creating one pass through stream because we only need "to send" once. The actual streaming is handled in another file or within our custom middleware handler using 'readline'. (Replace this with your use case)
      	    		   			     	     	// Send arrayStrings and headers to readableStream for processing later, without buffering all of them into memory at the time – you may want control flow in here.   // Assuming sending data back via HTTP response status code "201 Created" with a Content-Type header set as 'application/json'
      			   	   	    		     	// Use next request API to send headers for further use, such us: req['header']('x-poweredBy') = something; (replace accordingly); make sure you handle errors if required.  // Make stream available when user is done with their data and no more streaming in middleware
  	    		   	   			     	// Send the Readable Stream object to be sent back, without buffering it all at once using nextResponse function or directly sending out as file streams (this depends on your use case).  // Make sure you handle errors if required. The below code demonstrates a simple way of doing this in Nodejs
      	    		   	   			     	// You can find more about NextRequest here: https://nextjs.org/docs/api-routes/introduction (replace accordingly); modify as per your needs, add try catch blocks and other required errors handling code block to match it with the requirement above – provide a response if successful stream completion otherwise appropriate error message
      return new Response('OK', { status:201 }); // Returning HTTP Status Code "Created" in our case; replace 'OK' as per your needs. Also, modify headers here too (add more to set required header values).  If not needed with this specific code base structure just delete the response block if you want
    }catch(error){    	// Catch all error that may occur during middleware execution and provide appropriate message in catch part for handling such scenarios. Replace 'console' as per your needs (add more to handle different types of errors).  This implementation is very basic, add other functionalities if needed like logging the exceptions or sending notifications etc based on requirements
      console.log(error); // Assuming you want logs printed out in browser dev tools for troubleshooting purposes; replace 'console' as per your needs (add more to handle different types of errors).  If not required then delete this part if it is not needed with the current code base structure and requirement
      return NextResponse.json({ error: `Internal Server Error` }, { status:500 }); // Providing default server response when an unknown internal exception occurs (replace '{error :' Internal Severror'} as per your needs).  If you want a custom message for any specific errors, replace it with the appropriate HTTP Response Code and Message.
   }    		   	      	   	     	// Assuming sending data back via HTTP response status code "201 Created" or other suitable codes if not required (replace accordingly); make sure to handle cases like 4XX/5xx as per requirements; also modify headers here too, set appropriate values. If you want it done in a way which provides feedback about request's result without any redirect happening and is stateless ie no sessions or caching available then don’t include NextResponse if not required (remove the body block).
